title: Move Sales + UTM fields to their proper objects (additive first, dual-write, then cut over)
goal: Unblock revenue recognition. Today there are 0 sale fields on the Opportunity and 0 contacts with click IDs - so revenue cannot be recognized per transaction and channel attribution does not exist. Fix capture immediately so all NEW data flows to the right object; backfill historical separately.
inputs: Field destination plan from /to-be Field placement
prompt:
Goal: Stop the revenue-recognition bleed. New data starts flowing to the correct objects TODAY.

Two structural problems:
  RR-1: Sale Outcome + Product/Term/Price ×3 + Total Program Amount + Consultation Fee + Pay Type + nosale_reason all live on the Contact (1 per person). Per-deal revenue recognition impossible.
  RR-2: UTM ×5 + gclid + fbclid + source_url live on the Contact. Each new touch overwrites. Worse: gclid/fbclid are non-null on 0 of 11,077 contacts - capture is broken.

This is ADDITIVE-FIRST and DUAL-WRITE. Contact fields keep working; opportunity fields receive parallel writes. Cutover happens later.

VERIFY-THEN-CREATE (do not blind-create): the Opportunity object already carries a set of custom fields. Start with GET /customfields?model=opportunity, snapshot it, and create ONLY the fields from the s1c target list that are actually missing. Reconcile the counts before writing anything - the authoritative target set is defined in s1c-add-opp-fields (against pm-site-v2/public/data-spec.json), not a hardcoded number here.

Phase A - Reconcile + create only the MISSING opportunity-level fields from the s1c target set (additive)
Phase B - Copy the latest UTM + click IDs onto the Opportunity at create (attribution also stays on the Contact; no separate object)
Phase C - Fix click ID capture in workflows 01A–E (currently silently dropped)
Phase D - Dual-write: workflow 01 writes attribution to both Contact AND Opportunity. Sale OUTCOME is written by the PCC Sales Form only (single writer); WF-05 is a router that reacts to the form-written values and never writes an outcome itself.
Phase E - Drift validation: assert opp.sale_outcome count == contact.sale_outcome count after 7 days

DO NOT: delete contact fields (Step 6 does that), backfill historical opps (Step 2), or change workflow 05's auto-Won defect (separate P0).
validates: Opportunity has sale_outcome + monetaryValue set on new data
gclid/fbclid captured on a test ad click
No data loss on existing contact field reads
Drift check passes after 7 days
outputs: Missing opp fields created (verify-then-create against a fresh pull)
Attribution dual-write on WF 01; outcomes written by the PCC Sales Form only

title: Verify-then-create the Opportunity field set (sale outcome, products, prices, attribution copy, renewal, EMR FK)
goal: Make the Opportunity the single source of truth for deals - outcome, money, location, attribution. VERIFY-THEN-CREATE: the Opportunity object already carries custom fields, so pull the live set first and create only what is missing from the target list below (do not blind-create, do not assume a fixed count). Reconcile the field count before writing. Includes a copy of the lead's attribution (UTM + click IDs) on the Opportunity for revenue-to-campaign reporting, the renewal_date that arms WF-09 renewals, and the FK slot for EMR Visit. Renames patient_advisor to patient_care_consultant_id and ad_reason to nosale_reason as part of the same pass.
inputs: Prerequisite: S1a-create-lead-source done (need lead_source.id to reference as FK target)
Note: opportunity.monetary_value, .contact_id, .pipeline_id, .pipeline_stage_id, .status are GHL built-in - do NOT recreate
prompt:
Goal: Ensure the Opportunity object holds sale outcome, revenue, location, attribution, renewal, and the EMR back-reference. VERIFY-THEN-CREATE: create only the fields from the target list below that are missing. All additive - no existing field is changed in this prompt. Renames happen in a separate prompt (s1e-renames).

Pre-flight:
  1. GET /customfields?model=opportunity. Snapshot current state to audit/opp-fields-before.json.
  2. Reconcile: diff the live field set against the target list below. Emit migration/s1c-field-reconciliation.json listing {already_present, to_create}. G1b reported ~49 existing opportunity custom fields, so expect many targets to already exist - only create the genuinely missing ones. Never blind-create a duplicate key.

Step 1 - Create ONLY the missing target fields. For each, POST /customfields with model=opportunity. Use the EXACT key, name, dataType, and (for SINGLE_OPTIONS) values from data-spec.json objects[name="Opportunity"].fields[]. Categories:

  Attribution FK (1):
    opportunity.lead_source_id (TEXT) - FK lead_source.id (from S1a-create-lead-source). Attribution copy of UTM + gclid/fbclid lives in the attribution copy fields; this FK links the canonical lead source.

  Location & routing (1):
    opportunity.location (SINGLE_OPTIONS required) - values: [location_richmond, location_va_beach, location_npn, location_telehealth]

  Outcome & sale type (2):
    opportunity.sale_outcome (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [sold, no_sale, mut, mar, undecided]. The PCC Sales Form is the sole writer; WF-05 routes on it. (mar = Medical Approval Required: Opp stays Open, no automation fires.)
    opportunity.sale_type (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [new, renewal, upsell, reactivation]. REQUIRED on the form when sale_outcome=sold - WF-05 branches New vs Renewal on it.

  Renewal (1):
    opportunity.renewal_date (DATE) - set by WF-06 at Won; arms the WF-09 Renewal Reminders sub-flow.

  Ownership FKs (2):
    opportunity.patient_care_consultant_id (TEXT) - FK user.id. NEW key; legacy patient_advisor is renamed in s1e.
    opportunity.provider_making_recommendation_id (TEXT) - FK user.id.

  Products / Terms / Prices (9):
    opportunity.product_sold_1, product_sold_2, product_sold_3 (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [trt, ed, weight_mgmt, peptides, supplements, labs, other]
    opportunity.term_length_1, term_length_2, term_length_3 (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [1mo, 3mo, 6mo, 12mo, 24mo]
    opportunity.price_1, price_2, price_3 (MONETARY)

  Revenue (3):
    opportunity.total_program_amount (MONETARY) - WF 05 copies into monetary_value on Won.
    opportunity.consultation_fee (MONETARY)
    opportunity.pay_type (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [financing, cash, card, ach, hsa, fsa, insurance, other]

  No-sale (renamed; NEW keys here, legacy retired in Step 6):
    opportunity.nosale_reason (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [nosale_cost, nosale_fear, nosale_partner, nosale_timing, nosale_decision, nosale_medical, nosale_other]
    opportunity.nosale_reason_other (LARGE_TEXT)

  Cancellation / no-show (2):
    opportunity.cancellation_reason (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [scheduling_conflict, health_issue, no_longer_interested, found_alternative, other]
    opportunity.no_show_reason (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [forgot, scheduling_conflict, transportation, no_longer_interested, unreachable, other]

  Qualification (5):
    opportunity.primary_concern (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [ed, trt, weight, sleep, fatigue, performance, general_wellness, other]
    opportunity.age_range (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [under_30, 30_39, 40_49, 50_59, 60_69, 70_plus]
    opportunity.health_goal (LARGE_TEXT)
    opportunity.service_interested_in (MULTIPLE_OPTIONS)
    opportunity.preferred_appointment_date (DATE)

  Dispositions (3):
    opportunity.preferred_appointment_time (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [morning, midday, afternoon, evening]
    opportunity.call_disposition (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [connected, voicemail, no_answer, wrong_number, callback_requested, dnc, other]
    opportunity.consult_disposition (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [showed, no_show, cancelled, rescheduled]

  Notes & feedback (4):
    opportunity.lead_notes (LARGE_TEXT)
    opportunity.visit_feedback_score (NUMERICAL) - 1-10 NPS/CSAT.
    opportunity.visit_feedback_text (LARGE_TEXT)
    opportunity.review_status (SINGLE_OPTIONS) - values: [not_asked, asked, posted, declined]
    opportunity.review_link (TEXT)

  EMR back-reference (1):
    opportunity.emr_visit_id (TEXT) - FK external.emr.visit_id; one per visit.

Step 2 - Validation:
  a. GET /customfields?model=opportunity. Snapshot to audit/opp-fields-after.json. Assert every target key from the list is present (whether pre-existing or newly created).
  b. Diff before vs after: assert additions == count(to_create) from the reconciliation file, 0 modifications, 0 deletions.
  c. Test record: pick a test opportunity, PATCH each target field with a sample value. Assert all writes succeed.
  d. Idempotency: 2nd run reports 0 changes (all targets already present).

HARD rules:
  - DO NOT modify opportunity.monetary_value, .contact_id, .pipeline_id, .pipeline_stage_id, .status (built-in).
  - DO NOT delete or rename any existing custom field here. Renames (ad_* -> nosale_*, patient_advisor -> patient_care_consultant) happen in s1e.
  - DO NOT blind-create: if a target key already exists, leave it and record it in the reconciliation file. Never create a duplicate key.
  - DO NOT touch workflow 05 here. Wiring WF-05 to ROUTE on the form-written opportunity.sale_outcome is part of the P0 fix-bleed prompt + s4-wire-outcome. WF-05 never writes the outcome; the PCC Sales Form does.

Outputs:
  - migration/s1c-field-reconciliation.json (already_present vs to_create)
  - migration/s1c-opp-fields.json (field_ids for every target field)
  - audit/opp-fields-before.json + audit/opp-fields-after.json (diff baseline)
  - migration/s1c-opp-fields.log
validates: Every target opportunity field exists with exact spec keys + types (pre-existing or newly created)
sale_outcome enum includes mar; sale_type present for the WF-05 New/Renewal branch
opportunity.renewal_date + emr_visit_id slots ready
No existing opp field modified; no duplicate keys created
Test PATCH succeeds for each target field; script is idempotent
outputs: Diff snapshots + field IDs file for downstream prompts

title: Rename ad_* -> nosale_*, patient_advisor -> patient_care_consultant (dual-write, do NOT delete)
goal: Rename two fields that cause confusion. "A&D" looks like "advertising" but means Advise and Decline (a no-sale outcome) - if a future engineer keys off ad_* in WF 13 they would send real no-sales to Meta/Google as fake conversions. "PA" looks like Physician's Assistant but means Patient Care Consultant (PCC) (the sales role). Both renames write to the new field while keeping the old one live; the old fields are deleted in Step 6 after all readers migrate.
inputs: Snapshot of current contact.ad_reason, contact.ad_other_explanation, contact.patient_advisor values (back up before any change)
prompt:
Goal: Two rename operations, both additive + dual-write. The new fields on Opportunity already exist (added in S1c). This prompt copies values from the legacy Contact fields to the new Opportunity fields for the OPEN opportunity set, and stands up dual-write triggers so going forward both old and new locations stay in sync until Step 6 retires the old.

Rename 1: contact.ad_reason -> opportunity.nosale_reason
Rename 2: contact.ad_other_explanation -> opportunity.nosale_reason_other
Rename 3: contact.patient_advisor -> opportunity.patient_care_consultant_id

Why these matter:
  - WF 13 (Ad-Platform Conversions) fires Meta CAPI / Google Ads. If anyone keys off ad_* on a contact, they wire NO-SALES into AD CONVERSIONS - the worst possible signal-to-platform mistake. The rename eliminates the namespace collision.
  - In a healthcare context, "PA" = Physician's Assistant. The patient_advisor field is the sales role (Patient Care Consultant / PCC). Same character string, different professions. Rename for clarity.

Pre-flight:
  1. Confirm s1c-add-opp-fields.json exists; load opportunity.nosale_reason, nosale_reason_other, patient_care_consultant_id field_ids.
  2. Snapshot all three legacy Contact field values into audit/legacy-rename-snapshot.json (one row per contact that has any value set). This is the BACKUP - do not lose it.
  3. Inventory which workflows currently READ the legacy fields:
       grep -l "ad_reason\|ad_other_explanation\|patient_advisor" ghl_data/workflow_steps/*.json
     Write the result to migration/s1e-legacy-readers.txt. Each of these must be updated to read the new keys before Step 6 retires the old.

Step 1 - Copy legacy values forward (one-time backfill, OPEN opps only).
  For each Contact with a value in any of the three legacy fields:
    a. Find the most recent OPEN opportunity for that contact (status = open).
    b. If no open opp exists, skip and log to migration/s1e-orphans.json (no destination opp).
    c. PATCH the opp with:
         - opportunity.nosale_reason = mapped_value(contact.ad_reason)
         - opportunity.nosale_reason_other = contact.ad_other_explanation
         - opportunity.patient_care_consultant_id = contact.patient_advisor
    d. Mapping for nosale_reason values: see data-spec.json for the new value list. If the legacy value doesn't map, set to nosale_other and put the original value in nosale_reason_other.

  This is DRY-RUN first: emit a CSV (exports/s1e-rename-dryrun.csv) of every proposed PATCH. Owner reviews before write mode runs. Same gate as Step 2 backfill.

Step 2 - Dual-write going forward (no workflow changes here yet; this prompt only SETS UP the rule).
  Document in migration/s1e-dualwrite-spec.md exactly which workflow steps need updating in a follow-up prompt (s4-wire-renames):
    - WF 05 currently writes contact.ad_reason. Needs to ALSO write opportunity.nosale_reason on the related opp. Until Step 6, both writes happen.
    - Wherever contact.patient_advisor is currently set (likely WF 12 disposition, the 4a/4b dispositions), also write opportunity.patient_care_consultant_id on the related opp.
  Do NOT modify workflows here. Producing the spec doc is enough for s4-wire-renames to consume.

Step 3 - Validation:
  a. Re-read 100 random rows from the dry-run CSV; spot-check the contact values match what was proposed.
  b. Apply CSV to OPEN opps. Re-pull and assert opp values match CSV.
  c. Assert legacy Contact fields are still present + unchanged (we DID NOT delete them).
  d. Idempotent: 2nd run finds 0 contacts to backfill (all open opps already updated).

HARD rules:
  - DO NOT delete contact.ad_reason, contact.ad_other_explanation, contact.patient_advisor in this prompt. Step 6 Cleanup only, after readers migrate.
  - DO NOT touch CLOSED opportunities here. The historical Won/Lost backfill (Step 2) handles closed opps and rewrites them as part of the broader revenue restatement. This prompt only handles OPEN opps.
  - DO NOT trigger workflow enrollment via these PATCHes. Use non-triggering API endpoints (set fields directly). Verify execution-log count == 0 during the write window.

Outputs:
  - exports/s1e-rename-dryrun.csv (every proposed PATCH for human review)
  - exports/s1e-rename-applied.csv (after write mode confirms each)
  - audit/legacy-rename-snapshot.json (backup of every legacy value)
  - migration/s1e-orphans.json (contacts with legacy values but no open opp)
  - migration/s1e-legacy-readers.txt (workflow files that still read legacy fields - input for s4-wire-renames)
  - migration/s1e-dualwrite-spec.md (workflow change spec for s4-wire-renames)
validates: All OPEN opportunities have new opp-level values copied from legacy Contact fields
Legacy Contact fields untouched (Step 6 Cleanup deletes)
Dry-run CSV reviewed before write mode
Zero workflow enrollments triggered during PATCH window
100% of legacy readers identified for s4-wire-renames
Idempotent: 2nd run = 0 changes
outputs: Open opps updated with renamed field values
Legacy-reader inventory for the workflow-rewire prompt

title: Remove the auto-Won action from workflow 05
goal: Stop creating false Won opportunities on attendance. Move WF-05 to the router model: the PCC Sales Form is the sole writer of sale_outcome, sale_type, and value; WF-05 triggers on the form submission and reacts to those values (it never writes an outcome). New data is clean from day one even before the historical backfill runs.
inputs: Workflow 05 ID from inventory (resolve the real ID first: grep the workflow inventory for 'Clinic Appt Outcome' / 'A&D' and substitute it for wf05_id below - do NOT run against the literal placeholder)
prompt:
Goal: Remove the auto-Won defect from workflow 05 (Clinic Appt Outcome) and convert it into a router. Do not touch any other workflow.

Pre-flight: resolve the real workflow-05 ID from the inventory (search title 'Clinic Appt Outcome' / the A&D auto-Won workflow) and use it everywhere below in place of wf05_id.

Steps:
1. Read ghl_data/workflow_steps/<wf05_id>.json. Confirm the offending step exists.
2. Open workflow 05 in GHL UI via deeplink.
3. DELETE the single create_opportunity action named "A&D pipeline: Closed, status: WON - hide from a&D opportunity".
4. Change the TRIGGER to 'PCC Sales Form submitted'. WF-05 READS opportunity.sale_outcome + sale_type that the form already wrote; it does NOT write them. Build the router branches: Sold (require sale_type New|Renewal from the form) -> Won stage, set monetary_value = total_program_amount; No-Sale/Advised-and-Declined -> Lost; MUT -> Lost-MUT + suppress; MAR -> stay Open, tag mar. Add a parallel 'no form by end of appointment day' chaser that only nags the PCC and writes NO outcome fields.
5. Add the single-writer guardrail: enable Appointment/Opportunity 'Modified By' detection and an alert on any Sale Outcome change not originating from the PCC Sales Form (Risk H5).
6. Save as DRAFT.

Validation: re-extract; assert exactly one create_opportunity removed. Drive a test contact through SHOWED with NO form -> assert no outcome written and the PCC chaser fires. Submit the form as Sold+New -> assert WF-05 routes to Won and monetary_value is set. Assert no non-form write path can set Sale Outcome.
validates: A&D pipeline no longer receives new opps from WF 05
PCC Sales Form is the sole writer of sale_outcome/sale_type/value; WF-05 only routes
No-form chaser nags the PCC and writes no outcome
Modified-By alert fires on any non-form outcome change (H5)

title: Create target pipelines, opportunity fields, lost reasons, tags
goal: Stand up the additive structures the rest of the migration writes into. Nothing existing is modified - purely net-new objects.
prompt:
Goal: Create the additive foundation: 3 new pipelines, the opportunity field set (delegated to s1c), lost reasons, tag registry, Custom Values, a tag/field governance gate, and contact dedup preferences. Instagram DM pipeline already exists (4th, kept).

Pipelines: Sales - Lead to Close · Retention & Renewals · Referrals

Opportunity fields: do NOT hardcode a count here. The authoritative target set + creation logic is s1c-add-opp-fields (verify-then-create against a fresh GET /customfields?model=opportunity pull, reconciling against the ~49 fields that already exist). This prompt just confirms s1c has run and the target set is present.

Tag registry (case-sensitive, exact casing locked): source_*, location_*, funnel_entry_* (kept for attribution only - the timer branch, not a tag, drives WF-02 entry), status_*, objection_*, and the outcome_* family written by WF-05 (outcome_sold, outcome_ad, outcome_mut, outcome_mar).

Custom Values (Decision 6 - parameterize per location, do NOT clone workflows per location): create Custom Values for location address, location_phone, business hours, and the booking URL (BookMWC.com). Workflows and message bodies reference these Custom Values so one workflow serves all locations instead of 92-step per-location clones.

Governance gate (G4): stand up a tag/field governance checklist - new tags/fields must be registered here with owner + purpose before use; no ad-hoc tag creation in downstream workflows. Emit governance/tag-field-registry.md.

Contact dedup preferences: set the account dedup rule to Email primary, Phone secondary so referred/re-submitted contacts merge predictably (relevant to WF-15 NEW-contact creation).

Validation: re-pull pipelines + customfields. Assert 4 pipelines exist and the s1c target field set is present. Assert Custom Values resolve on a test send. Assert dedup preference is set. Idempotent - second run = 0 changes.
validates: 4 target pipelines exist
s1c opportunity field set present (verify-then-create, no hardcoded count)
Tag registry includes outcome_* family; casing locked
Custom Values for address/phone/hours/booking URL resolve
Governance gate + Email-primary dedup preference in place
Idempotent

title: Unify 9 legacy consent fields into canonical sms_consent_status (Decisions 5 + 8)
goal: Establish one source of truth for SMS consent. Today consent is spread across 9 fields in 2 schemas plus an orphan consent_log custom object, and 0 of 11,077 contacts have a stored value. This prompt reconciles all legacy consent signals into the single canonical Contact field sms_consent_status, and records the assume-consent accepted-risk decision (Decision 8) for the historical base. WF-11 is the single DND + consent authority thereafter; every SMS workflow filters on this field + native DND.
inputs: Inventory of the 9 legacy consent fields (sms_consent_status, sms_optin_date, consent_source, consent_timestamp, + the EMR-side consent fields)
consent_log custom object export (orphan, to be deleted in Step 6)
prompt:
Goal: Collapse all SMS-consent signals into the canonical Contact field sms_consent_status. VERIFY-THEN-WRITE, non-triggering, dry-run first.

Pre-flight:
  1. Snapshot all 9 legacy consent fields + the consent_log object to audit/consent-before.json. This is the backup.
  2. Confirm sms_consent_status exists as the canonical field (created/confirmed in p1-foundation).

Step 1 - Reconciliation map. For each contact, derive the canonical value by precedence: an explicit opt-out (STOP/DND) anywhere -> opted_out (opt-out always wins). Else any explicit opt-in signal -> opted_in with the earliest opt-in date. Else, per Decision 8 (assume-consent ACCEPTED RISK for the 11,077 historical contacts) -> assumed_consent. Emit exports/consent-reconciliation-dryrun.csv with the source signals and derived value per contact. Owner reviews before write mode.

Step 2 - Write mode (non-triggering). PATCH sms_consent_status directly via API (do NOT enroll workflows). Pause workflows during the window; assert execution-log sends == 0.

Step 3 - Guardrails live FIRST (Decision 8 rationale): confirm native DND/STOP handling is active and the 8 AM to 9 PM Contact-Timezone quiet-hours Time Window is applied on every SMS workflow before any assumed_consent contact can receive a send.

Step 4 - Do NOT delete the 9 legacy fields or the consent_log object here. Step 6 deletes consent_log after this reconciliation is confirmed. Record the assume-consent decision reference in the audit trail (owner Eric, 2026-07-10).

Validation: every contact has a non-null canonical sms_consent_status; opt-outs preserved (opted_out count >= legacy STOP/DND count); dry-run reviewed; zero sends during write window; idempotent second run = 0 changes.
validates: All 11,077 contacts have a canonical sms_consent_status value
Opt-outs preserved (opt-out always wins over any opt-in)
assumed_consent applied only where no explicit signal exists (Decision 8)
DND/STOP + 8a-9p quiet hours confirmed live before any assumed-consent send
Non-triggering write; idempotent
outputs: Canonical sms_consent_status populated; consent_log queued for Step 6 deletion

title: Generate the proposed reclassification CSV
goal: Produce a reviewable CSV that proposes: keep / split / reclassify for every Won opp. Writes nothing.
inputs: s1c opportunity field set present (sale_outcome, total_program_amount, etc.)
Export of all Won opps currently in the A&D pipeline
Owner review gate (CSV is reviewed before any write mode)
prompt:
Goal: Dry-run the revenue restatement. Produce a CSV; write nothing.


For each Won opp in A&D:
- Classify: clear-Won / clear-Lost / ambiguous
- Propose move (clear-Won → Sales · Won), Lost + lostReason for clear-Lost
- Ambiguous → human review queue, never auto-classified

Validation: Σ proposed monetaryValue reconciles against the opportunity values. count(Won)+count(Lost)+count(ambiguous) == count(A&D Won).
validates: Every Won opp classified
Ambiguous surfaced for human review
Sanity invariants hold

step: Step 3 - Build target workflows as drafts
title: Build the 16 target workflows in the GHL UI (drafts only)
goal: Construct each of the 16 target workflows as a draft in production.
inputs: Messaging copy (79 bodies, canonical WF-01..WF-16 numbering)
prompt:
Build all 16 target workflows as drafts. Do not publish.

For each: open the Workflows list, create from scratch, rename per target name, copy triggers/tags/bodies from source workflows. Reuse engine tag IDs. Reference Custom Values (address/phone/hours/BookMWC.com) instead of hardcoding per location.

QUIET HOURS (TCPA, applies to every SMS + voicemail workflow): wrap outbound sends in a Time Window of 8 AM to 9 PM in the Contact Timezone, and filter every send on canonical sms_consent_status = opted_in AND native DND = false. No SMS body may contain symptom or treatment specifics - appointment/consultation logistics only.

Special cases: WF 03 absorbs 03/3a/03b/03c/03d with G1 cadence (T-3d / T-1d / morning / T-2h) + telehealth branch; appointment-relative waits use an 'if date already passed' fallback. WF 05 is a router edited in place (form is sole writer). WF 13 trigger on Booked OR Won-with-monetaryValue>0; explicit EXCLUDE for A&D / No-Sale / Lost. WF 16 includes the simultaneous-ring IVR path.

Per-workflow validation: triggers fire on test contact, messages send in test mode, quiet-hours Time Window present on every messaging workflow, no accidental enrollment of production contacts.
validates: 16 drafts exist and pass test-contact runs
Every messaging workflow has an 8a-9p Contact-Timezone Time Window + consent/DND filter
No production enrollment during build

step: Step 4 - Dormant engine tag freeze + watch
title: Freeze the legacy engine tag surface and watch for drift before cutover
goal: Before cutover, put the legacy 'engine' tag surface (the tags that drive the old workflows) into a known frozen state and watch it, so nothing silently re-enrolls contacts or mutates tags while the new drafts sit ready. This is the gate between Step 3 (drafts built) and Step 5 (canary).
inputs: Engine tag inventory (chatbot_lead, src:bf-web, sniper_*, funnel_entry_*, etc.)
prompt:
Goal: Freeze and monitor the dormant legacy engine tag surface so the cutover starts from a stable base.

Step 1 - Snapshot the current engine tag surface (all engine tags + which workflows consume them) to audit/engine-tags-frozen.json.

Step 2 - Freeze: confirm no NEW automation writes engine tags outside the registry. Any workflow that still adds/removes engine tags must be paused or documented. Do NOT delete engine tags (they are protected surface; Step 6 keeps them).

Step 3 - Watch: stand up a daily drift check that diffs the live engine tag surface against the frozen snapshot and alerts on any unexpected add/remove/re-enrollment. Run it through the canary + 7-clean-day parallel run.

Validation: frozen snapshot captured; drift watcher runs and is quiet (0 unexpected changes) for the pre-cutover window; engine tags intact.
validates: Engine tag surface snapshot captured
No unregistered automation mutates engine tags
Drift watcher live and quiet before cutover
Engine tags not deleted

title: Wire WF-05 router, WF-10 survey -> visit_feedback_score, and WF-06 -> renewal_date
goal: Wire the outcome, feedback, and renewal signals the to-be workflows depend on but that do not exist yet. Enforces the single-writer model: the PCC Sales Form writes sale_outcome/sale_type/value; WF-05 only routes. Wires the WF-10 Feedback Survey to write opportunity.visit_feedback_score (which drives the WF-06 NPS branch), and WF-06 to set opportunity.renewal_date at Won (which arms the WF-09 Renewal Reminders sub-flow).
inputs: s1c fields present (sale_outcome incl. mar, sale_type, visit_feedback_score, renewal_date)
WF-05 draft (router), WF-06 draft, WF-10 draft
prompt:
Goal: Wire the three signals the to-be plan assumes exist.

WF-05 (router, single writer): confirm the PCC Sales Form writes opportunity.sale_outcome (sold|no_sale|mut|mar|undecided) + sale_type (required New|Renewal when sold) + total_program_amount. WF-05 TRIGGERS on the form submission, reads those values, and routes: Sold -> Won + monetary_value = total_program_amount + branch New->WF-06 / Renewal->WF-09 renewal sub-flow; No-Sale->WF-07; MUT->WF-11 suppress; MAR->stay Open, tag mar. WF-05 writes NO outcome. Add a Wait (30-60s) between any field write and a downstream If/Else read to avoid the race. Add the Modified-By alert on non-form outcome changes (H5).

WF-10 Feedback Survey: on survey submission, write opportunity.visit_feedback_score (1-10). This is the field the WF-06 NPS branch reads - without this wiring the NPS branch has no input.

WF-06 at Won: set opportunity.renewal_date (e.g., Won date + program term) so the WF-09 Renewal Reminders sub-flow has a date to fire relative to.

Validation: test form submission of each outcome routes correctly; sold-without-sale_type is rejected/flagged; survey submission populates visit_feedback_score and the WF-06 NPS branch fires on it; Won sets renewal_date and WF-09 renewal sub-flow arms. Race Wait present before every cross-read.
validates: PCC Sales Form is sole writer; WF-05 routes only
sale_outcome enum includes mar; sale_type enforced on Sold
WF-10 writes visit_feedback_score; WF-06 NPS branch reads it
WF-06 sets renewal_date; WF-09 renewal sub-flow arms
30-60s Wait between write and read (race fix)

title: Update workflow steps that read ad_*/patient_advisor to the new opp fields (consumes s1e spec)
goal: Migrate every workflow that still READS the legacy renamed fields (ad_reason, ad_other_explanation, patient_advisor) to the new Opportunity keys (nosale_reason, nosale_reason_other, patient_care_consultant_id), and stand up the dual-write steps documented by s1e. This must complete before Step 6 can delete the legacy fields.
inputs: s1c opportunity fields present (nosale_reason, nosale_reason_other, patient_care_consultant_id)
prompt:
Goal: Rewire readers + dual-writers for the renamed fields. Consumes the s1e outputs.

Step 1 - For each workflow file in migration/s1e-legacy-readers.txt, update the step to read the new Opportunity key instead of the legacy Contact key (ad_reason->nosale_reason, ad_other_explanation->nosale_reason_other, patient_advisor->patient_care_consultant_id).

Step 2 - Implement the dual-write steps from migration/s1e-dualwrite-spec.md: wherever a workflow set a legacy field, ALSO write the new opp field on the related opp, until Step 6 retires the legacy field. Note WF-13 (Ad Conversions) must key off outcome_* / Won-with-value, never ad_* (the namespace-collision hazard the rename fixes).

Step 3 - Validation: grep the workflow steps again for ad_reason / ad_other_explanation / patient_advisor reads -> expect 0 remaining legacy READS (dual-WRITES may remain until Step 6). Drive a test contact and assert the new opp fields are populated and read correctly.
validates: 0 remaining legacy-field reads across workflows
Dual-write steps live per s1e spec
WF-13 keys off outcome_*/Won-value, never ad_*
Test contact reads new opp fields correctly
outputs: Reader-migration checklist (input gate for Step 6 field deletion)

step: Step 5 - Canary cutover: 48h cohort, then 7 clean business days parallel run
title: 48h canary on src:bf-web cohort, then 7 clean business days of parallel run
goal: Publish 16 drafts; pause 38 sources. 48h canary on a test cohort, then widen and hold a 7-clean-business-day parallel run before Step 6 (Decision 10). Redesign-plus-migrate deviation is justified by the severity of the auto-Won defect; the extended parallel run offsets the added risk.
inputs: WF 11 confirmed live (consent + quiet hours as guardrails)
prompt:
Publish 16 drafts smallest-first; pause 38 source workflows. Add canary tag gate (src:bf-web).

Phase 1 - 48h canary on the src:bf-web cohort. Monitor: execution-log error rate < 0.1%, won-with-$0 = 0, no quiet-hours violations, no non-WF-05 (non-form) outcome writes, stage-flow sanity, SMS counts, STOP/DND replies.

Gate at 48h: clean -> widen to full volume; issues -> hold + document.

Phase 2 - after widening, run 7 CLEAN BUSINESS DAYS of parallel run. 'Clean' = error rate < 0.1%, won-with-$0 = 0, zero quiet-hours violations, zero outcome writes from anything other than the PCC Sales Form. Any dirty day resets the counter. Only after 7 consecutive clean business days + owner approval does Step 6 cleanup unlock.
validates: 48h canary clean, then 7 clean business days parallel run
Won-with-$0 stays 0; no quiet-hours violations
No non-form outcome writes; no double-execution

title: Retire old workflows/fields/pipelines + explicit retirements after 7 clean business days
goal: Make the new world the only world. Order by irreversibility. Only runs after 7 clean business days since cutover (Decision 10).
prompt:
Phase A: archive paused workflows (low risk).

Explicit retirements (Decision 9 - dropped deliberately, not silently orphaned). After confirming no live dependency, retire:
  - '06. Price Calculator' workflow
  - the standalone 'Intake Reminder' workflow (WF-04 owns all intake chasing going forward)
  - the Vercel 'Outbound Webhook'
Document each retirement (what, why, dependency check result) in migration/retirements.md.

Consent reconciliation cleanup (Decision 5): after consent-reconciliation has unified the 9 legacy consent fields into canonical sms_consent_status, DELETE the orphan consent_log custom object.

Google Sheets: for any Google Sheets integration still referenced, CONFIRM it is no longer needed (ask the owner, check for live reads) BEFORE deleting - do not assume.

Phase B: confirm no reads of ad_*, patient_advisor, or the never-populated fields -> DELETE them (irreversible - last).
Phase C: confirm pipeline opp counts == 0 -> DELETE retired pipelines.

NEVER delete engine tag surface (chatbot_lead, src:bf-web, sniper_*, funnel_entry_*).
validates: Explicit retirements (Price Calculator, Intake Reminder, Outbound Webhook) done with dependency check
Orphan consent_log object deleted after reconciliation
Google Sheets confirmed unneeded before any delete
No reads of retired fields; no opps in retired pipelines; engine tags intact
